samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. 8 16 A register to store the input enable signal mul_en_in. It has a width of 2 bits. A register to store the input multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. A register to store the input multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. A register to store the intermediate sum values with a data width of 16 bits. A register to store the accumulated sum with a data width of 16 bits.

mul_en_out is the output enable signal that indicates whether the multiplication operation is enabled. It is derived from the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg.

On the positive edge of the clock signal, the input enable signal mul_en_in is sampled and stored in mul_en_out_reg.

mul_en_out is assigned the value of the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg.

mul_a_reg is a register that stores the input multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits.

mul_b_reg is a register that stores the input multiplier with a data width of 8 bits.

On the positive edge of the clock signal, if the input enable signal mul_en_in is active, the input multiplicand and multiplier are stored in mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg.

temp is a set of wires that stores the partial products generated from the bitwise AND operations between the bits of the multiplicand and the multiplier.

sum is a set of registers that stores the intermediate sum values for the partial products.

On the positive edge of the clock signal, if the input enable signal mul_en_in is active, the partial products are stored in sum.

mul_out_reg is a register that stores the accumulated sum of the partial products.

On the positive edge of the clock signal, mul_out_reg is updated with the sum of all the partial sums.

On the positive edge of the clock signal, if the output enable signal mul_en_out is active, the value of mul_out_reg is assigned to the output product mul_out.

Otherwise, mul_out is set to 0.
